trade.summary: Indonesia exports coal, LNG, palm oil, and minerals to South Korea. South Korea exports electronics, automobiles, steel, and chemicals. The ASEAN-Korea FTA provides a preferential framework. South Korea's EV battery supply chain increasingly depends on Indonesian nickel.

diplomatic.summary: Elevated to 'Special Strategic Partnership' in 2017. Both countries cooperate through ASEAN+3, G20, and bilateral summits. South Korea's 'New Southern Policy' has deepened engagement with ASEAN, with Indonesia as a priority country. Cultural ties (Korean Wave, K-pop) are also significant.

military.summary: South Korea and Indonesia signed a landmark defence cooperation deal including the KF-21 Boramae fighter jet co-development programme. Indonesia is a 20% co-development partner in the KF-21, representing a major defence industrial partnership. Joint exercises are also conducted.

financial.summary: South Korean FDI in Indonesia has surged, particularly in the EV battery value chain (POSCO, LG, Samsung SDI). Korean companies are investing in Indonesian nickel processing and battery manufacturing. Construction of the new Indonesian capital Nusantara has attracted Korean firms.

energy.summary: Indonesia is a key coal and LNG supplier to South Korea. Cooperation on renewable energy and green hydrogen is growing. Korean investment in Indonesian geothermal energy has also occurred.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific and technological cooperation, particularly in defence R&D (KF-21 programme), EV battery technology, and smart city development. Korean universities and research institutes are increasing engagement with Indonesian partners.
